figures.caption: Figure 1: Task overview. Given egocentric observations and a text prompt, EgoPriMo generates full-body motion for humanoid control.
Table 2: Unified task evaluation on Nymeria. EgoPriMo uses one checkpoint for all tasks; UniEgoMotion is evaluated under the matched protocol. Lower is better except for SemSim.

Figure 3: Qualitative egocentric motion generation results. Each example pairs egocentric observations with generated SMPL full-body motion, illustrating how EgoPriMo maps egocentric scene cues to temporally coherent body configurations.

Figure 4: Humanoid robot control with EgoPriMo. The model generates SMPL-based full-body motions from egocentric observations and text prompts; an existing Unitree whole-body controller tracks the generated references on the real platform.

Figure 2: Overview of EgoPriMo. Egocentric observations, auxiliary pose cues, and text prompts are routed through task-conditioning masks into a Triple-stream DiT motion model. Under different conditioning settings, the same model supports motion reconstruction, forecasting, and generation. The generated SMPL-based full-body motion can further serve as a control reference for a humanoid robot.
